---
title: "DA research"
author: "Nancy Tran"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-18"
institute: "Denison University"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: lux # Check here for more themes: https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-themes.html
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Code"
    code-copy: hover
    link-external-newwindow: true
    tbl-cap-location: top
    fig-cap-location: bottom

self-contained: true
editor: source
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# DO NOT EDIT THIS

knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.align = 'center')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(out.width = '90%')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(results = 'hold')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.show = 'hold')
knitr::opts_chunk$set(error = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
par(mar = c(4.1, 4.1, 1.1, 4.1))

hooks = knitr::knit_hooks$get()
hook_foldable = function(type) {
  force(type)
  function(x, options) {
    res = hooks[[type]](x, options)
    
    if (isFALSE(options[[paste0("fold.", type)]])) return(res)
    
    paste0(
      "<details open><summary>", gsub("^p", "P", gsub("^o", "O", type)), "</summary>\n\n",
      res,
      "\n\n</details>"
    )
  }
}

knitr::knit_hooks$set(
  output = hook_foldable("output"),
  plot = hook_foldable("plot")
)

```

# Import Library

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(sf)
library(caret)
library(ranger)
library(xgboost)
library(Matrix)
library(ggplot2)
library(rgee)
library(cowplot)
#install.packages("geodata")
library(geodata)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(soilDB)
library(tidyr)
#install.packages("exactextractr")
```

# Dataset
```{r}

soc_data <- read.csv("Data/clean1.csv", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)

#factoe long and lat --> numeric
soc_data$lon<-as.numeric(soc_data$Longtitude)
soc_data$lat<-as.numeric(soc_data$Latitude)

pts_sf <- st_as_sf(soc_data, coords = c("lon","lat"),crs=4326) #what does 4326 mean?
```

#chelsa
```{r}
urls <- readLines("Data/envidatS3paths.txt")
target_urls <- urls[grepl("bio1_|bio12_", urls)]
for (u in target_urls) {
  dest <- file.path("data/chelsa", basename(u))
  if (!file.exists(dest)) download.file(u, dest, mode="wb")
}

# Load Chelsa Rasters
r_bio1 <-rast("../CHELSA_bio1_1981-2010_V.2.1.tif")
r_bio12 <- rast("../CHELSA_bio12_1981-2010_V.2.1.tif")
# Stack them
bio <- c(r_bio1, r_bio12)
names(bio) <- c("bio1_temp", "bio12_precip")
#Extract & merge with original data
vals <- terra::extract(bio, vect(pts_sf))
soc_data_climate <- cbind(soc_data, vals)
```

After the above code chunk, the annual average temperature and annual average 

```{r}

table(soc_data_climate$depth)
barplot(table(soc_data_climate$depth))

```
Depth:
- 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

soc_data_climate <- soc_data_climate %>%
  # split depth into lower/upper bounds
  mutate(depth_low  = as.numeric(str_extract(depth, "^[0-9]+")),
         depth_high = as.numeric(str_extract(depth, "[0-9]+$")),
         depth_mid  = (depth_low + depth_high) / 2) %>%
  # classify into bins
  mutate(depth_class = case_when(
    depth_mid <= 30 ~ "0-30",
    depth_mid > 30 & depth_mid <= 60 ~ "30-60",
    depth_mid > 60 ~ "60+",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

```

# Elevation 
Data is extracted from https://bigdata.cgiar.org/srtm-90m-digital-elevation-database/ 

```{r}
# the srtm files are downloaded from CSI CGAR (90m)
dem1<-rast("../srtm1.tif")
dem2<-rast("../srtm2.tif")
dem3<-rast("../srtm3.tif")
points<-data.frame(id=soc_data_climate$id,x=soc_data_climate$lon,y=soc_data_climate$lat)

pts_vec<-vect(points,geom=c("x","y"),crs="EPSG:4326")

e1 <- terra::extract(dem1, pts_vec)[,2]
e2 <- terra::extract(dem2, pts_vec)[,2]
e3 <- terra::extract(dem3, pts_vec)[,2]

# combine dem 1, 2, 3 into the elevation column
soc_data_climate$elev <- dplyr::coalesce(e1, e2, e3)

# add combine the two elevation column
soc_data_climate$average.elevation<-as.numeric(soc_data_climate$average.elevation)
soc_data_climate$elev <- coalesce(soc_data_climate$elev,
                                  soc_data_climate$average.elevation)
```

```{r}
library(tidyr)
# Select numeric columns you want
plot_data <- soc_data_climate %>%
  select(elev, bio1_temp, bio12_precip) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(elev, bio1_temp, bio12_precip),
               names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")

# histogram faceted by variable
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal()
```

At lower elevation with warmer temperatures, chemical weathering processes such as hydrolysus and oxidation tend to me more rapid. This results in deeper soils rich in clay minerals, and secondary oxides --> more microbial activity, orgamic matter decomposes faster and is incorporated into mineral soil horizons

```{r}
# ---- Slope ----
# compute slope rasters for each DEM (units = degrees by default)
#slope1 <- terrain(dem1, v = "slope", unit = "degrees")
#slope2 <- terrain(dem2, v = "slope", unit = "degrees")
#slope3 <- terrain(dem3, v = "slope", unit = "degrees")

#s1 <- terra::extract(slope1, pts_vec)[,2]
#s2 <- terra::extract(slope2, pts_vec)[,2]
#s3 <- terra::extract(slope3, pts_vec)[,2]

# combine slope values (use first non-NA from s1, s2, s3)
#soc_data_climate$slope <- coalesce(s1, s2, s3)
```

# Convert SOC unit
```{r}
# Convert SOC units 
soc_data_climate$SOC_gKg <- with(soc_data_climate,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "%", soc.value * 10,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "g_kg", soc.value,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "g_g", soc.value * 1000,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "mg_g", soc.value,                # 1 mg/g = 1 g/kg
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "mg_kg", soc.value / 1000, NA))))))
 
soc_data_climate$SOC_MgHa <- with(soc_data_climate,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "Mg_ha", soc.value,
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "g_m2", soc.value * 0.01,   # 1 g/m² = 0.01 Mg/ha
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "kg_m2", soc.value * 10,    # 1 kg/m² = 10 Mg/ha
  ifelse(soc.value_units == "kg_ha", soc.value / 1000,  # 1 kg/ha = 0.001 Mg/ha
  NA)))))
```

# Log Transformation the SOC unit

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# inspect
summary(soc_data_climate$SOC_MgHa)
summary(soc_data_climate$SOC_gKg)

# define a safe log transform function
safe_log <- function(x, add = NULL) {
  # add: if NULL, compute minimal shift so all values > 0
  if (is.null(add)) {
    minv <- min(x[is.finite(x)], na.rm = TRUE)
    # if minv <= 0, choose shift = abs(minv) + tiny
    add <- ifelse(minv <= 0, abs(minv) + 1e-6, 0)
  }
  log(x + add)
}

# create transformed vars in dataframe
soc_unit <- soc_data_climate %>%
  mutate(
    SOC_MgHa_finite = ifelse(is.finite(SOC_MgHa), SOC_MgHa, NA_real_),
    SOC_gKg_finite = ifelse(is.finite(SOC_gKg), SOC_gKg, NA_real_)
  )
# pick one to transform; here we transform SOC_MgHa (stock)
# if there are zeros/negatives, we use safe_log to shift
min_SOC_mgha <- min(soc_data$SOC_MgHa_finite, na.rm = TRUE)
min_SOC_gkg <- min(soc_data$SOC_gkg_finite, na.rm = TRUE)
shift_mgha <- ifelse(min_SOC <= 0, abs(min_SOC_mgha) + 1e-6, 0)
shift_gkg <- ifelse(min_SOC <= 0, abs(min_SOC_gkg) + 1e-6, 0)

soc_unit <- soc_unit %>%
  mutate(
    log_SOC_MgHa = safe_log(SOC_MgHa_finite, add = shift_mgha),
    log_SOC_gKg = safe_log(SOC_gKg_finite, add = shift_gkg)
  )
summary(soc_unit$log_SOC_MgHa)
summary(soc_unit$log_SOC_gKg)

```

```{r}
# packages
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)   # gives p-values
library(performance) # for check_collinearity etc.

# prepare variables
soc_main <- soc_unit %>%
  mutate(
    depth_class = factor(depth_class, ordered = FALSE),
    main_descriptor = factor(main_descriptor),
    labmethod = factor(labmethod),
    crop=factor(crop),
    # scale continuous predictors for easier interpretation
    elevation_s = scale(elev),
    temp_s = scale(bio1_temp),
    precip_s = scale(bio12_precip)
  )

# simple LMM: log_SOC_MgHa predicted by descriptors + site random intercept
mod_lmm1 <- lmer(log_SOC_MgHa ~ depth_class + elevation_s + temp_s + precip_s + labmethod + main_descriptor +
                  (1 | id), data = soc_main, REML = FALSE)

summary(mod_lmm1)
anova(mod_lmm1)        # type I tests
# for Type III F-tests you can use car::Anova on the lmerTest model:
library(car)
Anova(mod_lmm1, type = "III")

# check collinearity
check_collinearity(mod_lmm1)

# optionally add random slopes if warranted:
mod_lmm2 <- lmer(log_SOC_MgHa ~ depth_class + elevation_s + temp_s + precip_s + labmethod + main_descriptor +
                  (1 + exp_year | id), data = soc_main, REML = FALSE)
summary(mod_lmm2) #--> there is no different between year / elevation

anova(mod_lmm1, mod_lmm2)  # compare via likelihood ratio test
# residuals, qq-plot
plot(mod_lmm1)    # residuals vs fitted
qqnorm(resid(mod_lmm1)); qqline(resid(mod_lmm1))

# better: DHARMa for residual diagnostics (handles LMM residual complexities)
library(DHARMa)
simres <- simulateResiduals(mod_lmm1)
plot(simres)

```

```{r}
barplot(table(soc_data_climate$depth_class))
```

```{r}
barplot(table(soc_data_climate$year))
```

```{r}
barplot(table(soc_data_climate$exp_year))
```

```{r}
ggplot(soc_data_climate, aes(x = country)) +
  geom_bar()+theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
crop_counts <- soc_data_climate %>%
  count(crop, sort = TRUE)

ggplot(crop_counts %>% slice_max(n, n = 20), aes(x = reorder(crop, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Crop / System", y = "Count", title = "Top 20 Reported Crops/Systems") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
soc <- soc_data_climate %>%
  select(SOC_gKg, SOC_MgHa) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), 
               names_to = "variable", 
               values_to = "value")
ggplot(soc, aes(x = variable, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "SOC Variable", y = "Value", title = "SOC distributions") +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
hier <- soc_data_climate %>%
  count(main_descriptor, secondary_descriptor, additional_descriptor, name = "n") %>%
  ungroup()

library(treemapify)

ggplot(hier, aes(area = n, fill = main_descriptor,
                 subgroup = secondary_descriptor, label = additional_descriptor)) +
  geom_treemap() +
  geom_treemap_subgroup_border() +
  geom_treemap_subgroup_text(
    place = "centre", 
    colour = "white", 
    size = 10,       # fixed font size (try 2–4 depending on plot size)
    grow = FALSE,   # prevent scaling by box size
    fixed = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_treemap_text(
    aes(label = paste(additional_descriptor, "\n", n)),
    place = "topleft",
    size = 7,     # fixed font size
    grow = FALSE,
    reflow = TRUE,
    fixed = TRUE
  )


ggplot(hier, aes(area = n, fill = main_descriptor,
               label = additional_descriptor, subgroup = secondary_descriptor)) +
  geom_treemap() +
  geom_treemap_subgroup_border(colour = "white", size =0.6) +
  geom_treemap_subgroup_text(place = "centre",size=10, grow = FALSE,
                             alpha = 0.25, colour = "black",
                             fontface = "italic", fixed=FALSE) +
  geom_treemap_text(colour = "white", place = "topleft",
                    size = 7, grow = FALSE,fixed = FALSE) 
```

# Add thickness
```{r}
parse_depth <- function(depth_str) {
  if(is.na(depth_str) || depth_str == "") {
    return(list(top = NA_real_, bottom = NA_real_, thick = NA_real_))
  }
  s <- as.character(depth_str)
  # find numbers (works for most common formats)
  nums <- as.numeric(str_extract_all(s, "\\d+\\.*\\d*")[[1]])
  if(length(nums) >= 2) {
    top <- nums[1]; bottom <- nums[2]
  } else if(length(nums) == 1) {
    # treat single number as bottom with top = 0 if plausible, otherwise keep NA
    top <- 0; bottom <- nums[1]
  } else {
    return(list(top = NA_real_, bottom = NA_real_, thick = NA_real_))
  }
  thick <- bottom - top
  if(is.na(thick) || thick <= 0) thick <- NA_real_
  return(list(top = top, bottom = bottom, thick = thick))
}

# ---- apply to your dataframe (assume it's soc_data_climate and depth col is 'depth') ----
# parse depths into numeric columns top, bottom, thickness
depth_parsed <- lapply(soc_data_climate$depth, parse_depth)

depth_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(depth_parsed, function(x) data.frame(top=x$top, bottom=x$bottom, thick=x$thick)))
soc_data_climate$thickness <- depth_df$thick
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(point, aes(x = lon, y = lat)) + geom_point()
```
```{r}
# Example: Hanoi
lon <- 105.8
lat <- 21.0

url <- paste0("https://rest.isric.org/soilgrids/v2.0/properties/query?",
              "lon=", lon, "&lat=", lat, "&property=clay,sand,silt,bdod",
              "&depth=0-5cm")

jsonlite::fromJSON(url)

```


```{r}
library(terra)
r <- bio  # SpatRaster from WorldClim
# buffer extraction example
library(sf)
pts_sf <- st_as_sf(points, coords = c("lon","lat"), crs = 4326)
pts_buf <- st_buffer(pts_sf, dist = 1000)  # 1 km buffer (in degrees? prefer projected CRS)
# Reproject to metric CRS first
pts_buf_m <- st_transform(pts_buf, 3857)
# extract mean over buffer (use exactextractr for polygons)
library(exactextractr)
vals_buffer <- exactextractr::exact_extract(r, pts_buf_m, fun='mean')

```


```{r}
# ----- 1. Country-level summaries for mapping -----
# Make sure columns names exist; adjust strings if necessary
temp_col <- "average.annual.temperature"
precip_col <- "average.annual.precipitation"
country_col <- "country"

if (!all(c(temp_col, precip_col, country_col) %in% names(ml_data))) {
  stop("One of the required columns (temperature/precipitation/country) is missing. Check column names.")
}

#This code create a subset of 9 countries in Southeast Asia with their mean temperature and precipitation, sample size
country_summary <- ml_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(.data[[country_col]])) %>%
  group_by(country = .data[[country_col]]) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_temp = mean(.data[[temp_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_precip = mean(.data[[precip_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    country_low = tolower(country)
  )

# ----- 2. Create world map data & join by country name (case-insensitive) -----
world_map <- map_data("world") %>%
  mutate(region_low = tolower(region))

# Quick helper to map some common name differences (you can expand as needed)
synonyms <- tribble::tribble(
  ~mapname,             ~countryname,
  "myanmar",            "myanmar",     # maps uses 'myanmar'
  "laos",               "laos",
  "timor-leste",        "timor-leste",
  "brunei darussalam",  "brunei",
  "vietnam",            "vietnam",
  "philippines",        "philippines",
  "malaysia",           "malaysia",
  "indonesia",          "indonesia",
  "thailand",           "thailand",
  "cambodia",           "cambodia",
  "singapore",          "singapore"
)
# Create a lookup: map region name -> country summary (if exists)
# We'll left_join by region_low == country_low; missing will be NA and plotted gray.

map_df <- world_map %>%
  left_join(country_summary, by = c("region_low" = "country_low"))

# Restrict plotting window roughly to Southeast Asia long/lat bounds:
lon_limits <- c(90, 155)
lat_limits <- c(-13, 25)
```


```{r}
# ----- 3. Precipitation map -----
p_precip <- ggplot(map_df, aes(long, lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(fill = mean_precip), color = "gray60", size = 0.2) +
  coord_quickmap(xlim = lon_limits, ylim = lat_limits) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "C", na.value = "gray95",
                     name = "Avg annual\nprecipitation (mm)") +
  labs(title = "Country-average annual precipitation (dataset)",
       subtitle = "Values are dataset means for points in each country") +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
p_precip
```


```{r}
p_temp<-ggplot(map_df, aes(long, lat, group = group)) +
  geom_polygon(aes(fill = mean_temp), color = "gray60", size = 0.2) +
  coord_quickmap(xlim = lon_limits, ylim = lat_limits) +
  scale_fill_viridis(option = "A", na.value = "gray95",
                     name = "Avg annual\ntemperature (°C)") +
  labs(title = "Country-average annual temperature (dataset)",
       subtitle = "Values are dataset means for points in each country") +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
p_temp
```


```{r}
# Save maps
ggsave("map_avg_precip.png", p_precip, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("map_avg_temp.png", p_temp, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# ----- 5. Prepare features for ML (RF and XGBoost) -----
# Choose predictors you mentioned (adjust list if your column names differ)
predictors <- c("thickness", "topDepth",
                "average.annual.precipitation", "average.annual.temperature",
                "labmethod", "tillage", "organic_amend", "fertilization", "crop_system", "country")

# Keep only available predictors
predictors <- intersect(predictors, names(ml_data))
cat("Using predictors:", paste(predictors, collapse = ", "), "\n")

ml_df <- ml_data %>%
  select(SOC_MgHa, all_of(predictors)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(SOC_MgHa))  # ensure target present

# Convert categorical predictors to factors
cat_vars <- predictors[sapply(ml_df[predictors], function(x) !is.numeric(x))]
ml_df[cat_vars] <- lapply(ml_df[cat_vars], as.factor)

# Quick numeric imputation (median) for any numeric NAs among chosen predictors:
num_vars <- intersect(predictors, names(ml_df))[sapply(ml_df[predictors], is.numeric)]
for (v in num_vars) {
  na_idx <- is.na(ml_df[[v]])
  if (any(na_idx)) {
    ml_df[[v]][na_idx] <- median(ml_df[[v]], na.rm = TRUE)
    message("Imputed median for ", v, " (", sum(na_idx), " NAs).")
  }
}
```


```{r}
# ----- 6. Random Forest (ranger) fit on full data for variable importance -----
set.seed(42)
rf_fit <- ranger::ranger(
  formula = SOC_MgHa ~.,
  data = ml_df,
  importance = "permutation",
  num.trees = 500
)
rf_imp <- as.data.frame(rf_fit$variable.importance, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
rf_imp <- tibble::rownames_to_column(rf_imp, var = "feature")
colnames(rf_imp)[2] <- "importance"
rf_imp <- rf_imp %>% arrange(desc(importance))
```


```{r}
# ----- 7. XGBoost fit (we need a numeric model matrix) -----
# Create model matrix (one-hot for categorical)
mm <- model.matrix(SOC_MgHa ~ . - 1, data = ml_df)  # drops intercept
dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = mm, label = ml_df$SOC_MgHa)

xgb_params <- list(objective = "reg:squarederror",
                   max_depth = 6, eta = 0.05, subsample = 0.8, colsample_bytree = 0.8)
set.seed(42)
xgb_nrounds <- 200
xgb_mod <- xgb.train(params = xgb_params, data = dtrain, nrounds = xgb_nrounds, verbose = 0)

xgb_imp <- xgb.importance(model = xgb_mod)
# xgb_imp is a data.frame with columns Feature, Gain, Cover, Frequency
xgb_imp <- xgb_imp %>% arrange(desc(Gain))
```


```{r}
# ----- 8. Get top 5 predictors from each model -----
top5_rf <- rf_imp %>% slice_max(order_by = importance, n = 5)
top5_xgb <- xgb_imp %>% slice_max(order_by = Gain, n = 5)
```


```{r}
# ----- 9. Friendly renaming function for readability -----
pretty_name <- function(var) {
  # handle model.matrix dummy names like 'crop_systemrice' or 'countryThailand'
  v <- as.character(var)
  # Common full-name replacements
  v <- gsub("^average\\.annual\\.precipitation$", "Avg annual precipitation (mm)", v)
  v <- gsub("^average\\.annual\\.temperature$", "Avg annual temperature (°C)", v)
  v <- gsub("^thickness$", "Sampling thickness (cm)", v)
  v <- gsub("^topDepth$", "Top depth (cm)", v)
  # handle prefixes:
  v <- gsub("^labmethod", "Lab method ", v)
  v <- gsub("^tillage", "Tillage ", v)
  v <- gsub("^organic_amend", "Organic amendment ", v)
  v <- gsub("^fertilization", "Fertilization ", v)
  v <- gsub("^crop_system", "Crop system ", v)
  v <- gsub("^country", "Country ", v)
  # model.matrix style: replace '.' or '_' with space and clean
  v <- gsub("\\.", " ", v)
  v <- gsub("_", " ", v)
  # tidy up multiple spaces
  v <- gsub(" +", " ", v)
  # trim
  v <- trimws(v)
  # Capitalize first letter
  v <- paste0(toupper(substr(v,1,1)), substr(v,2,nchar(v)))
  return(v)
}
```


```{r}
# Build data frames for plotting
rf_plot_df <- top5_rf %>%
  mutate(pretty = sapply(feature, pretty_name)) %>%
  arrange(importance)  # for horizontal bar order

xgb_plot_df <- top5_xgb %>%
  rename(importance = Gain, feature = Feature) %>%
  mutate(pretty = sapply(feature, pretty_name)) %>%
  arrange(importance)
```


```{r}
# ----- 10. Plot top-5 bars (horizontal) -----
p_rf <- ggplot(rf_plot_df, aes(x = reorder(pretty, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#1f78b4", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest: Top 5 predictors",
       x = "", y = "Permutation importance") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
p_rf
```


```{r}
p_xgb <- ggplot(xgb_plot_df, aes(x = reorder(pretty, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#33a02c", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "XGBoost: Top 5 predictors",
       x = "", y = "Importance (gain)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
p_xgb
```


```{r}
# Save plots
ggsave("rf_top5.png", p_rf, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
ggsave("xgb_top5.png", p_xgb, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# Optionally combine map(s) and importance plots in one figure for the paper
library(cowplot)
combined <- plot_grid(p_rf, p_xgb,
                      labels = c("A", "B"),
                      ncol = 2, align = "hv")
combined

library(patchwork)

combined <- (p_precip + theme(legend.position = "right") | 
             p_temp + theme(legend.position = "right")) /
            (p_rf | p_xgb)

combined + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")

#ggsave("combined_figure.png", combined, width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300)

#cat("All done. Outputs saved:\n - map_avg_precip.png\n - map_avg_temp.png\n - rf_top5.png\n - xgb_top5.png\n - combined_figure.png\n")

```

## upper code ends here

```{r}
soc_data <-read.csv("SOC_dataset.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# 1. coords fallback
soc_data <- soc_data %>%
  mutate(
    rep.lat = as.numeric(rep.lat),
    rep.long = as.numeric(rep.long),
    near.lat = as.numeric(near.lat),
    near.long = as.numeric(near.long),
    rep.lat = ifelse(is.na(rep.lat), near.lat, rep.lat),
    rep.long = ifelse(is.na(rep.long), near.long, rep.long)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(rep.lat) & !is.na(rep.long))
```

```{r}
area_unit <- c("Mg_ha","kg_ha","kg_m2","g_m2")
area_unit_data <- subset(soc_data, soc.value_units %in% area_unit)

# Convert SOC units to Mg/ha
area_unit_data$SOC_MgHa <- with(area_unit_data, ifelse(soc.value_units =="Mg_ha", soc.value,
                                   ifelse(soc.value_units =="kg_ha", soc.value/1000,
                                          ifelse(soc.value_units =="kg_m2", soc.value*10,
                                                 ifelse(soc.value_units =="g_m2", soc.value*0.1, NA)))))

#Drop rows with NA just to make sure
area_unit_data <-subset(area_unit_data,!is.na(SOC_MgHa))
```

```{r}
# 5. Depth parse (assuming depth like "0-10")
parse_depth <- function(d){
  if(is.na(d)) return(list(top=NA_real_, thickness=NA_real_))
  s <- gsub("cm","", as.character(d))
  if(grepl("-", s)){
    p <- str_split(s, "-")[[1]] %>% as.numeric()
    return(list(top = p[1], thickness = p[2] - p[1]))
  } else if(grepl("\\+", s)){
    top <- as.numeric(gsub("\\+","", s))
    return(list(top = top, thickness = NA_real_))
  } else return(list(top=NA_real_, thickness=NA_real_))
}
depths <- lapply(area_unit_data$depth, parse_depth)
area_unit_data$topDepth <- sapply(depths, `[[`, "top")
area_unit_data$thickness <- sapply(depths, `[[`, "thickness")
```

# 6. Text-clean helpers

```{r}
clean_text <- function(x) tolower(trimws(as.character(ifelse(is.na(x), "", x))))

area_unit_data <- area_unit_data %>%
  mutate(
    main_clean = clean_text(main_descriptor),
    sec_clean  = clean_text(secondary_descriptor),
    add_clean  = clean_text(additional_descriptor),
    crop_clean = clean_text(crop)
  )
```

# 7. Classify tillage, organic amendments, fertilization, crop system

```{r}
area_unit_data <- area_unit_data %>%
  mutate(
    tillage = case_when(
      str_detect(sec_clean, "no[- ]?till|zero[- ]?till|conservation") | str_detect(add_clean, "no[- ]?till") ~ "no-till",
      str_detect(sec_clean, "reduced|minimum|min[- ]?till") ~ "reduced",
      str_detect(sec_clean, "conventional|plough|plow|till") ~ "conventional",
      TRUE ~ "unknown"
    ),
    organic_amend = case_when(
      str_detect(add_clean, "manure|compost|residu|mulch|straw|biosolid|leaf litter") | str_detect(sec_clean, "manure|compost") ~ "yes",
      str_detect(add_clean, "without fertilization|no amendment|no residue") ~ "no",
      TRUE ~ "unknown"
    ),
    fertilization = case_when(
      str_detect(add_clean, "\\b(fertilizer|npk|urea|ammonium|nitrate|phosphate|kcl|chemical)\\b") ~ "synthetic",
      str_detect(add_clean, "\\b(manure|compost|organic)\\b") ~ "organic",
      str_detect(add_clean, "without fertilization|no fertilization|no fertilizer") ~ "none",
      TRUE ~ "unknown"
    ),
    crop_system = case_when(
      str_detect(main_clean, "agroforestry") | str_detect(crop_clean, "agroforestry") ~ "agroforestry",
      str_detect(main_clean, "perennial|plantation") | str_detect(crop_clean, "oil palm|rubber") ~ "perennial",
      str_detect(main_clean, "annual|cropping") | str_detect(crop_clean, "rice|maize|soy") ~ ifelse(str_detect(crop_clean, "rice"), "rice", "annual"),
      TRUE ~ "other"
    )
  )
```

# 8. Impute climate variables by country median then global median

```{r}
temp_col <- names(area_unit_data)[grepl("temperature", names(area_unit_data), ignore.case=TRUE)][1]
prec_col <- names(area_unit_data)[grepl("precip", names(area_unit_data), ignore.case=TRUE)][1]

if(!is.null(temp_col)) area_unit_data[[temp_col]] <- as.numeric(area_unit_data[[temp_col]])
if(!is.null(prec_col)) area_unit_data[[prec_col]] <- as.numeric(area_unit_data[[prec_col]])

if("country" %in% names(area_unit_data)){
  for(col in c(temp_col, prec_col)){
    if(!is.null(col)){
      area_unit_data[[col]] <- ave(area_unit_data[[col]], area_unit_data$country, FUN = function(x) { x[is.na(x)] <- median(x, na.rm=TRUE); x })
      area_unit_data[[col]][is.na(area_unit_data[[col]])] <- median(area_unit_data[[col]], na.rm=TRUE)
    }
  }
} else {
  for(col in c(temp_col, prec_col)){
    if(!is.null(col)) area_unit_data[[col]][is.na(area_unit_data[[col]])] <- median(area_unit_data[[col]], na.rm=TRUE)
  }
}
```

# not do yet
```{r}
# 9. Save
write_csv(ml_data, "ML_data.csv")
cat("Saved SOC_dataset_BC_cleaned.csv with", nrow(df), "rows\n")

```

```{r}
soc_data_spatial <- soc_data_spatial %>%
  select(SOC_MgHa, main_descriptor, secondary_descriptor, additional_descriptor,
         average.annual.temperature, average.annual.precipitation,
         topDepth, thickness, rep.lat, rep.long)
```


```{r}
# ml_spatial_rf_xgb.R
# Reproduce ML on cleaned 840 rows using only selected variables
# Required packages: dplyr, readr, sf, caret, ranger, xgboost, Matrix, ggplot2


# ---- 1. load cleaned file ----
data_path <- "SOC_dataset_BC_cleaned.csv"  # path to the cleaned 840-row CSV you already have
df <- read_csv(data_path, guess_max = 20000)
cat("Rows loaded:", nrow(df), "\n")

# ---- 2. select variables you specified (rename to match your file if needed) ----
# update these names to match exact column names in your csv
temp_col <- c("average.annual.temperature") %>% intersect(names(df)) %>% first()
prec_col <- c("average annual precipitation", "average.annual.precipitation") %>% intersect(names(df)) %>% first()

num_vars <- c("thickness", "topDepth", "average.annual.temperature", "average.annual.precipitation") %>% intersect(names(area_unit_data))
cat_vars <- c("labmethod","tillage","organic_amend","fertilization","crop_system","country","main_clean","sec_clean","add_clean","crop_clean") %>% intersect(names(area_unit_data))

# check
cat("Using numeric:", paste(num_vars, collapse=", "), "\n")
cat("Using categorical:", paste(cat_vars, collapse=", "), "\n")

# subset and drop rows missing target or coords
ml_data <- area_unit_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(SOC_MgHa)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(rep.lat) & !is.na(rep.long)) %>%
  select(SOC_MgHa, rep.long, rep.lat, all_of(num_vars), all_of(cat_vars))

cat("Rows available for modelling:", nrow(ml_data), "\n")
```


```{r}
# ---- 3. impute numeric with median if any NAs remain ----
for (v in num_vars) {
  if (any(is.na(ml_data[[v]]))) {
    med <- median(ml_data[[v]], na.rm = TRUE)
    ml_data[[v]][is.na(ml_data[[v]])] <- med
    cat("Imputed median for", v, "=", med, "\n")
  }
}
```


```{r}
# ---- 4. reduce cardinality of categorical variables (keep top K levels) ----
reduce_card <- function(x, K = 12) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x[is.na(x) | x==""] <- "Unknown"
  top <- names(sort(table(x), decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(K, length(unique(x)))]
  x[!x %in% top] <- "Other"
  factor(x)
}
for (v in cat_vars) ml_data[[v]] <- reduce_card(ml_data[[v]], K = 12)
```


```{r}
# ---- 5. create spatial clusters (kmeans on coords) -> use as group folds ----
coords <- as.matrix(ml_data[, c("rep.long", "rep.lat")])
set.seed(42)
k <- 5  # number of spatial folds (adjustable)
km <- kmeans(coords, centers = k, nstart = 20)
ml_data$.sp_cluster <- factor(km$cluster)
table(ml_data$.sp_cluster)
```


```{r}
# ---- 6. prepare training matrices ----
# numeric matrix
X_num <- as.matrix(ml_data[, num_vars, drop = FALSE])

# categorical one-hot (sparse)
if (length(cat_vars) > 0) {
  dummies <- model.matrix(~ . - 1, data = ml_data[, cat_vars, drop = FALSE])
  X_cat <- as.matrix(dummies)
} else {
  X_cat <- matrix(nrow = nrow(ml_data), ncol = 0)
}

X <- cbind(X_num, X_cat)
y <- ml_data$SOC_MgHa
```


```{r}
# ---- 7. spatial cross-validation using group folds (GroupKFold emulation) ----
groups <- ml_data$.sp_cluster
unique_groups <- unique(groups)

spatial_cv <- function(model_type = c("ranger", "xgb"), params = list()) {
  preds <- numeric(length(y))
  rmses <- c(); r2s <- c()
  for (g in unique_groups) {
    train_idx <- which(groups != g)
    test_idx  <- which(groups == g)
    Xtr <- X[train_idx, , drop=FALSE]; ytr <- y[train_idx]
    Xte <- X[test_idx, , drop=FALSE]; yte <- y[test_idx]
    if (model_type == "ranger") {
      df_tr <- data.frame(y = ytr, Xtr)
      rf <- ranger::ranger(y ~ ., data = df_tr,
                           num.trees = params$num.trees %||% 300,
                           max.depth = params$max.depth %||% 12,
                           mtry = params$mtry %||% floor(ncol(Xtr)/3),
                           importance = "permutation")
      p <- predict(rf, data = as.data.frame(Xte))$predictions
    } else if (model_type == "xgb") {
      dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = Xtr, label = ytr)
      watch <- list(train = dtrain)
      bst <- xgb.train(params = list(objective = "reg:squarederror",
                                     max_depth = params$max.depth %||% 6,
                                     eta = params$eta %||% 0.05,
                                     subsample = params$subsample %||% 0.8,
                                     colsample_bytree = params$colsample_bytree %||% 0.8),
                       data = dtrain, nrounds = params$nrounds %||% 300, verbose = 0)
      p <- predict(bst, xgb.DMatrix(Xte))
    }
    preds[test_idx] <- p
    rmses <- c(rmses, sqrt(mean((yte - p)^2)))
    r2s   <- c(r2s, cor(yte, p)^2)
  }
  list(preds = preds, rmse_mean = mean(rmses), rmse_sd = sd(rmses), r2_mean = mean(r2s), r2_sd = sd(r2s))
}
```


```{r}
# ---- 8. run RF and XGBoost spatial CV ----
cat("Running RF spatial CV (this may take a minute)...\n")
rf_res <- spatial_cv("ranger", params = list(num.trees = 300, max.depth = 12))
cat(sprintf("RF RMSE = %.3f, R2 = %.3f\n", rf_res$rmse_mean, rf_res$r2_mean))
```


```{r}
cat("Running XGBoost spatial CV...\n")
xgb_res <- spatial_cv("xgb", params = list(nrounds = 300, max.depth = 6, eta = 0.05))
cat(sprintf("XGB RMSE = %.3f, R2 = %.3f\n", xgb_res$rmse_mean, xgb_res$r2_mean))
```


```{r}
# ---- 9. fit final RF on full dataset and compute permutation importance ----
df_full <- data.frame(y = y, X)
final_rf <- ranger::ranger(y ~ ., data = df_full, num.trees = 500, max.depth = 12, importance = "permutation")
imp <- importance(final_rf)
imp_df <- data.frame(feature = names(imp), importance = imp) %>% arrange(desc(importance))
write.csv(imp_df, "feature_importance_cleaned.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r}
# ---- 10. plots: observed vs predicted (RF spatial CV) ----
obs <- y; pred_rf <- rf_res$preds
png("obs_vs_pred_rf_cleaned.png", width = 700, height = 700)
plot(obs, pred_rf, pch=20, xlab="Observed SOC_MgHa", ylab="Predicted (RF, spatial CV)")
abline(0,1, col="red", lty=2)
dev.off()
cat("Saved plots and feature importance to working directory.\n")

```
